name: Federal Reserve
summary: Trapped in stagflation — oil inflation ($112/bbl) too hot to cut, war-driven economic weakness too fragile to hike. $200B Pentagon supplemental request compounds fiscal concerns. All three major central banks (Fed, ECB, RBI) frozen simultaneously = global monetary policy paralysis. Rates held at 3.50-3.75%. Section 122 tariffs adding 0.5-0.6% to price level.

signals.content: FOMC voted 11-1 to hold rates at 3.50-3.75%. Projected one rate reduction in 2026 and another in 2027. But markets don't believe even one cut.
Market removed basically every rate cut for 2026, now pricing odds of a HIKE. Global bond yields at highest since May 2024. Pandemonium in bond markets.

Fed remains trapped — 10Y at 4.44%, oil at $112.57, VIX at 31.05. Can't cut (inflation), can't hike (growth risk). ECB froze rate cuts March 19. RBI holding at 5.25%. All three major central banks on hold = global monetary policy paralysis. $200B Pentagon supplemental adds fiscal pressure. Section 122 tariffs adding 0.5-0.6% to price level (Yale Budget Lab). Vance: war will continue 'a little while longer' — no near-term resolution to Fed's trap.

edges.context: Fed policy → bond yields. 10Y at 4.37% (highest since Jul 2025). Markets pricing hike possibility. Three straight weeks of bond losses.
Higher rates → higher discount rates → equity valuations compressed. Fed unable to provide support as inflation concern dominates.
Strong dollar from rate expectations crushed gold (-10% worst week since 2011). Counter-intuitive: war usually helps gold but not when dollar strengthens.
Oil spike is the transmission mechanism from war to inflation to Fed policy. Oil above $100 = inflation stays elevated = Fed can't cut.
$200B war spending request compounds fiscal situation. War spending + no rate cuts = stagflation risk.

trigger_points.mechanism: Would crash equities globally. EM currencies including INR would face massive outflows. Gold would paradoxically benefit as recession fears rise.
